description: Every baked good uses one or more leavening systems. Matching the right system to the recipe is critical for texture.

systems.used_in: biszkopt, genoise, angel food, pound cake
systems.how: air bubbles trapped in protein/fat matrix expand during baking
systems.failure: deflates if batter sits too long or folded aggressively

systems.source: baking powder (double-acting: reacts with moisture + heat)
systems.how: CO2 released in two stages: mixing and oven heat
systems.failure: too much = bitter taste, fallen center; too little = dense

systems.source: baking soda requires acid (buttermilk, yogurt, lemon, cocoa)
systems.how: reacts immediately with acid — bake right after mixing
systems.ratio: 0.5 tsp (2.5g) per 125g flour with acid present
systems.failure: no acid = soapy taste; too much = brown surface, hollow

systems.how: fermentation produces CO2 and alcohol over time
systems.failure: too hot kills yeast; too cold = very slow rise; sugar >20% inhibits

systems.used_in: croissant, pâte à choux, puff pastry
systems.how: water turns to steam at 100°C, expanding layers
systems.failure: low oven temp = steam escapes slowly, no puff

description: Baking temperature determines crust formation, internal set, and color. Different baked goods require different temperature strategies.

guide.note: low temp = even rise, no crack
egg wash before baking for color
steam in first 10 min for crust
high heat = steam separation of layers

doneness_tests.biszkopt: toothpick comes out clean; top springs back when pressed
doneness_tests.drożdżówka: internal temp 88-92°C; sounds hollow when tapped on bottom
doneness_tests.sernik: edges set, center jiggles like jelly when shaken
doneness_tests.chleb: internal temp 95-98°C; sounds hollow when tapped
doneness_tests.kruche: pale gold color, edges slightly darker

description: Flour protein content directly determines gluten strength and texture. Wrong flour type is a common cause of baking failures.

types.use: biszkopt, kruche, delicate cakes — low gluten = tender
sourdough, rustic bread — strong gluten network
whole wheat bread, adds fiber and flavor

critical_rule: For biszkopt ALWAYS use mąka tortowa typ 450 — higher protein flour = tough rubbery sponge

description: Eggs serve multiple functions simultaneously. Understanding each function helps diagnose failures.

yolks.emulsification: lecithin in yolk emulsifies fat+water — creates smooth batter and tender crumb
yolks.richness: fat content adds moisture and flavor

whites.foam: proteins trap air when whipped — mechanical leavening
whites.structure: proteins coagulate at 62°C — set and stabilize
whites.drying: high water content can dry out baked goods if overused

temperature_note: room temperature eggs (20°C) whip to 30% more volume than cold eggs — always bring eggs to room temp before making biszkopt
